title: Slide/teaching-deck export for educators (from the explainer source)
context: Educators who want to teach from the stats-for-clinicians explainers in a lecture or workshop setting need a slide-deck export they can use directly, without having to manually convert Markdown explainers to presentation format. This backlog task adds a slide/teaching-deck export pipeline that generates a presentation-ready deck from the explainer source, deriving from the same prose and figures used in the Markdown version — no new statistical content, just a new output format. This task depends on adoption demand to justify the investment; it should not be started until >= 1 educator has expressed a need for this format.
objective: Build and merge a slide/teaching-deck export pipeline that generates presentation-ready decks from the explainer source files, using the same prose and figures without introducing new statistical content, licensed CC-BY for the content.
acceptanceCriteria: A pipeline generates presentation-ready slide decks from the explainer Markdown source using a standard open format (e.g. reveal.js HTML, Marp, or equivalent); the same figures from the reproducibility harness are reused without regenerating from scratch.
The pipeline does not introduce new statistical content; it is a rendering/format transformation of approved, biostatistician-signed-off explainer content.
The structural 'educational, not clinical advice' framing is present on the first and last slide of each deck.
Figures in the slide deck retain their alt-text and colorblind-safe palettes from the source explainer.
The deck source is CC-BY-4.0; the pipeline code is MIT; CI generates the deck as an artifact and verifies it renders without errors.
At least one educator has confirmed they would use this format before the task is started (demand-gate documented in the PR description).
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; CI green; commit DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md — Scope (slide/teaching-deck export listed as in-scope)
Open presentation frameworks: reveal.js, Marp, or equivalent
output: A merged MIT-licensed PR containing a slide/teaching-deck export pipeline that generates CC-BY-4.0 decks from explainer source files, with CI artifact generation, accessibility maintained, and the 'not clinical advice' frame on every deck.
